Cookies consent

We use cookies to ensure that we give you the best experience on our website.

Necessary cookies

Some cookies are required to provide core functionality. The website might not function properly without these cookies, they are enabled by default and cannot be disabled.

Intersnack IT KG
_cs_consent•Necessary

Stores the user's cookie consent state for the current domain.

Optional cookies

Enables Apply with Indeed feature. The Apply with Indeed feature allows you to quickly apply using an Indeed Resume. For details about the cookies used and the data received see Indeed's Privacy Policy. Apply with Indeed is subject to Indeed's Privacy Policy.

Enables Apply with LinkedIn feature. The Apply with LinkedIn feature allows you to apply for jobs by using your LinkedIn profile. When you click Apply with LinkedIn on our site, we'll pre-populate some of the job application fields with your profile data and automatically attach your complete LinkedIn profile to the application form. This means that we will be able to easily access your complete LinkedIn profile as we look through your application. For details about the cookies used and the data received see LinkedIn's Cookie Policy. Apply with LinkedIn is subject to LinkedIn's Cookie Policy.

Enables Apply with XING feature. The Apply with XING feature allows you to apply for jobs by using your XING profile. When you click Apply with XING on our site, we'll pre-populate some of the job application fields with your profile data and automatically attach your complete XING profile to the application form. This means that we will be able to easily access your complete XING profile as we look through your application. For details about the cookies used and the data received see XING's Cookie Policy. Apply with XING is subject to XING's Cookie Policy.

Intersnack IT KGHomepage

Privacy Policy

We, the Intersnack IT KG, would like to inform you on this website about our company. The protection of personal data and the legally compliant processing of such data are a concern we are strongly committed to.

In the following, we would like to give you a brief overview of how we handle personal data when you visit our website. The following information relates exclusively to the website ﻿https://intersnackitkg.recruitee.com/﻿ and its subpages. The data protection declaration does not apply to websites linked to from this website, in particular websites of career platforms like for example, but not limited to, LinkedIn and Indeed.

1.) Data controller and Data Protection Officer

The controller for the processing of personal data is:

Intersnack IT KG Klaus-Bungert-Str. 8/8a 40468 Düsseldorf, Germany Phone: +49 211 710 65113 E-Mail: datenschutz(at)intersnack-it.com

You can reach our Data Protection Officer as follows:

Boris Reibach Scheja & Partners GmbH & Co. KG Adenauerallee 136 53113 Bonn Telefon: 0228/2272260 Encrypted contact form: ﻿﻿https://www.scheja-partners.de/en/contact/contact.html ﻿

2.) Applicants´ Data

Submitted application documents and other data collected in the course of the application procedure are personal data within the meaning of Art. 4 no. 1 of the EU-General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

Personal data is all information that relates to you as a person. We process the personal data provided by you and transmitted to us as part of your application, which generally includes

  • First and last name, salutation and title

  • Your contact details: Contact information such as address, telephone number, e-mail address, professional position if applicable

  • Your application data, consisting of your cover letter, CV and the usual supporting documents and certificates.

During the application process, additional data besides the application documents you submitted may be collected from you personally, from publicly available sources or from former employers and trainers.

3.) Purposes of processing applicants´ data

The legal basis for data processing in the course of the recruitment process or the execution of an employment relationship is, Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR.

If you consent in us saving your data for another position, the processing of your application data is based on your consent, which you can revoke at any time, Art. 6 (1) (a) and Art. 7 (3) GDPR.

Your application data will be used by us to assess if you can be a candidate

  • for the position for which you have expressly applied or

  • another position, if it was an unsolicited application or your qualification matches with another position than the one you applied for.

4.) Processing of applicants´ data

We process your application data using the following means:

  • in our in-house e-mail system

  • Recruitee (career software)

  • if necessary, additionally in paper form (printouts or copies).

We will forward your application data

  • to your possible future supervisor.

  • to the management if you apply for a managerial position

  • to affiliated companies of Intersnack Group GmbH & Co. KG, only after you have consented to that.

If the application procedure does not lead to your employment, we will delete and destroy your application data eight months after you or our company has finally rejected your application, unless you consent that we save your contact details for other interesting job offer.

If you ask us to save your contact details, the processing is based on your consent, which you can revoke at any time, Art. 6 (1) (a) and Art. 7 (3) GDPR.

Should an application procedure lead to an employment, we will include your application documents in your personnel file on the basis of Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR in order to inform about your personality profile and qualifications for the purpose of the performance of the employment relationship. In this case, your application documents will not be deleted and destroyed until your employment has been terminated and until the legal retention period has expired since the end of the year of termination.

5.) Cooperation with Third Parties

Not all data processing processes connected to recruiting are carried out in-house, but in some cases work together with external service providers:

  • Career software: We use Recruitee (www.recruitee.com) to process applicants´ data submitted to us.

  • Career platforms: We use career platforms like Indeed, LinkedIn and Xing to place job offers and to look for candidates.

Please note: The processing of data in your profile on a career platform will be carried out by the provider of that career platform and is subject to the Terms and Conditions and Privacy Information of that career platform. We cannot influence such processing of your data on a career platform.

Job Offers on Career Platforms

We place our job offers on career platforms, especially Indeed, Xing and LinkedIn. If you click on our job offer, you will be forwarded to our job advertisement on our career site, which is currently hosted by Recruitee.

The following data will be collected from you and forwarded to us:

  • Source website, i.e. the website where you found the job offer

The following data might be collected by the provider of our career site, but will not be forwarded to us

  • Your device and browser

  • Your IP-address

  • Requests and responses sent from and to your device

This data might be used by the provider of our career site for:

  • Learning more about the behaviour of the visitors of careers website to improve the recruitment process and the website;

  • Improving and monitoring the performance of the website;

You are furthermore free to decide whether you would like to use your profile on career platforms like Indeed, Xing or LinkedIn to submit application data relevant If you decide to do so, your profile information stored on the career platform will be linked to our job offer via an interface and your profile data will be transferred to the Recruitee Software we use.

6.) Contacting you via a Career Platform

It might be the case that we contact you via a career platform (Indeed, Xing or LinkedIn). In this event we use your information offered within your profile on the career platform and ask you whether you are interested in a specific position.

If you are interested in a position, we will handle your personal data as described above.

7.) Your Rights

As a data subject, you may contact us or our Data Protection Officer at any time with an informal note to exercise your rights under the GDPR (contact details above under point 1). These rights are the following:

  • The right to obtain information about the data processing, as well as a copy of the processed data (right of access, Art. 15 DSGVO),

  • the right to request the correction of inaccurate data or the completion of incomplete data (right to rectification, Art. 16 GDPR),

  • the right to request the erasure of personal data and, if the personal data have been made public, to inform other data controllers of the request for erasure (right to erasure, Art. 17 GDPR),

  • the right to request the restriction of data processing (right to restriction of processing, Art. 18 GDPR),

  • the right to receive the personal data of the data subject in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format and to request the transfer of such data to another controller (right to data portability, Art. 20 DSGVO),

  • the right to object to data processing based on a consideration of interests pursuant to Art. 6 para. 1 sentence 1 lit. f) DSGVO in order to stop it (right to object, Art. 21 DSGVO),

  • the right to withdraw a given consent at any time in order to stop data processing based on your consent. The withdrawal does not affect the lawfulness of the processing based on the consent prior to the withdrawal (right of withdrawal, Art. 7 DSGVO).

You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority responsible for us if you believe that the data processing violates the GDPR (right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority, Art. 77 GDPR). This is the

Landesbeauftragte für Datenschutz und Informationsfreiheit Nordrhein-Westfalen Postfach 20 04 44 40102 Dusseldorf Germany Phone: +49211/38424-0 Fax: +49211/38424-999 E-Mail: poststelle@ldi.nrw.de

Information for the Processing of Applicant Data

Submitted application documents and other data collected in the course of the application procedure that can be personally assigned to you as an applicant are protected personal data within the meaning of Art. 4 No. 1 of the EU-General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). We hereby inform you about the details of the processing of your applicant data in accordance with Art. 13 GDPR.

Your personal data is processed by our company exclusively within the scope of data protection regulations, in particular the GDPR and the German Federal Data Protection Act ('Bundesdatenschutzgesetz' - BDSG). Your personal data may only be collected, stored, disseminated or used (data processing, Art. 4 No. 2 GDPR) if this is expressly permitted or ordered by law or if you have given your effective consent (Art. 6 (1) subpara. 1 point (a), Art. 7 GDPR). Processing of your personal data is particularly permitted if this is necessary for the recruitment or after the establishment of an employment relationship for its performance or termination (Sec. 26 (1) BDSG, Art. 6 (1) subpara.1 point (b) GDPR). The same applies if data processing is necessary for the purposes of our legitimate interests other than the employment relationship and there is no reason to assume that your legitimate interests as data subject override our legitimate interests (Art. 6 (1) subpara.1 point (f) GDPR).

In cases where the applicant has no visa and sponsorship is needed, necessary personal data will be shared with a third party (localyze) to review eligibility of future work permit and residence permit. 

Controller and Contact

Controller (Art. 4 No. 7 GDPR) for the processing of your personal data is Intersnack IT KG, Klaus-Bungert-Str. 8/8a, 40468 Düsseldorf.

If you have any questions regarding data protection, please do not hesitate to contact us:

Human Resources Management: ﻿﻿hrservices@intersnack-it.com﻿

or

our external Data Protection Officer: datenschutz@intersnack-it.com﻿

Personal Data and Purposes of Processing

1. Application for an advertised Position

In order to be able to involve you in the application process for a specific position, we require application documents that are customary and meaningful, which inform us of your personality profile and qualifications. We only use your application documents to decide on filling the position for which you have expressly applied. For this purpose, we will evaluate your application documents in the HR department and forward them to your possible future supervisor. If you apply for a managerial position, the management will also receive your application documents. We process your application documents electronically in our email system, via Recruitee (recruiting platform) and, if necessary, additionally by using printouts or copies (paper form). During the application process, further personal data may be collected from you personally, from publicly available sources or from former employers and trainers for this purpose. The legal basis for data processing is Art. 6 (1) subpara.1 point (b) GDPR, Sec. 26 (1) BDSG. If the application procedure does not lead to your employment, we will delete and destroy your application data six months after you or our company has finally rejected your application.

Should an application procedure lead to an employment, we will include your application documents in your personnel file on the basis of Art. 6 (1) subpara.1 point (b) GDPR, Sec. 26 (1) BDSG in order to inform about your personality profile and qualifications for the purpose of the performance of the employment relationship. In this case, your application documents will not be deleted and destroyed until your employment has been terminated and until the legal retention period has expired since the end of the year of termination.

2. Unsolicited Applications

If you submit an unsolicited application that does not relate to a specific position, we can consult your application documents in the context of recruitment decisions for all possible positions. To this end, we will first evaluate your application documents in the HR department and then involve colleagues from the responsible department(s) within the Intersnack IT. We process your application documents electronically in our email system, via Recruitee and, if necessary, additionally by using printouts or copies (paper form). Once your application documents have been included in a recruitment process, we may collect additional personal data from you personally, from publicly available sources or from former employers and trainers to provide us with more detailed information about your personality profile and qualifications. We will regularly delete and destroy your application data after a period of one year from receipt of your application, but not earlier than six months following your or our company's final rejection of all application procedures to which your application documents have been consulted.

Should an application procedure lead to an employment, we will include your application documents in your personnel file on the basis of Art. 6 (1) subpara.1 point (b) GDPR, Sec. 26 (1) BDSG in order to inform about your personality profile and qualifications for the purpose of the performance of the employment relationship. In this case, your application documents will not be deleted and destroyed until your employment has been terminated and until the legal retention period has expired since the end of the year of termination.

3. Information about your career platform usage data

We place our job advertisements on career platforms, especially LinkedIn. If you find a job advertisement from us there and click on it, you will be forwarded to our job advertisement. The following data will be collected from you and forwarded to us:

  • Your device and browser

  • Your IP-address

  • requests and responsessent from and to your device

  • Source websites (https://recruitee.com/privacy)

We use your personal data for:

  • Learning more about the behaviour of the visitors of our careers website to improve our recruitment process and this website;

  • Improving and monitoring the performance of this website;

All processing of personal data of careers website visitors is based on our legitimate interest to facilitate recruitment (Art. 6 (1) subpara.1 point (f) GDPR).

You are furthermore free to decide in a next step, whether you would like to use your LinkedIn profile for your application with us. If you decide to do so, your profile information stored there will be linked to our offer via an interface and your profile data will be transferred to us. This is a voluntary offer to you, based on your consent and our legitimate interest (Art. 6 (1) subpara.1 point (a), (f) GDPR).

If you decide to use your profile from another career platform, this career platform might process your personal data as well. We are not informed about this and cannot influence such processing. For further information, please contact the third party:

LinkedIn Ireland Unlimited Company

4. If we contact you

It might be the case that we contact you via a career platform (LinkedIn). In this event we use your information offered within your profile on the career platform which we will store within our software offered by Recruitee (see no. 3.) and ask you whether you are interested in a pecific position. If this is the case, we will proceed as described above. If this is not the case, we will delete your contact data from our database immediately, unless you ask us to save your contact details for other interesting job offers (consent). This processing of personal data is based on our legitimate interest to facilitate recruitment (Art. 6 (1) subpara.1 point (f) GDPR). If you ask us to save your contact details, the processing is based on your consent, which you can revoke at any time (Art. 6 (1) subpara.1 point (a) GDPR).

4.1 Logfiles

For the purely informative use of the website, i.e. if you do not sign up or transmit any other information to us, only the data that your browser automatically transmits to the server on which the contents are hosted is stored in so-called logfiles and processed as required. If you view our website, we collect the following data, which is required technically by us in order to display our website to you and to ensure stability and security (the legal basis being Article 6 subs.1 (f) of the GDPR):

  • The IP address of the requesting computer

  • The date and time of the server request

  • The URL of the respectively accessed website

  • Where applicable, the URL of the website via which the user has gone to the accessed website

  • The name of the requested file

  • The page from which the file has been requested

  • The access status of the web server (file transferred, file not found, command not executed, etc.)       

  • The browser type and version

  • The operating system of the terminal device used

  • The data volume transferred

The collection of this data takes place in an anonymized form only. The data collected therefore does not allow any conclusions to be drawn as to specific persons. There is no merging of this data with other data sources that could possibly create a reference to a person. At the most, the data is evaluated for statistical purposes and is automatically deleted after four weeks.

The servers on which www.intersnack-it.com is hosted, and on which the personal data processed during your visit to our website is stored, are located in Germany and are operated by CANCOM Pironet AG & Co. KG (Von-der-Wettern-Straße 27, 51149 Köln) in its capacity as the hosting service provider.

Our Cooperation with Third Parties

We also benefit from the advantages of a society based on the division of labour and the corporate world. In the area of data processing, this means that we do not carry out all data processing processes in-house, but in some cases work together with external service providers:

  • Career software: in order to offer and use the technical possibilities described above (connection to the career platforms), we work together with Recruitee (www.recruitee.com), a software and cloud provider that we have carefully selected and integrated in compliance with data protection law

  • Career platforms: in order to find you, we will also place our job advertisements on external career platforms (e.g. LinkedIn) via Recruitee. If you apply through them, they may forward/store your documents and usage data to us as described above (Personal Data and Purposes of Processing; 3. Information about your career platform usage data).

  • External (IT) service providers that we have contractually obligated as so-called processors to process data trictly according to our specifications and instructions and to protect this data comprehensively both technically and organisationally. For the cloud-based handling of the recruiting process we have concluded a data processing agreement with Recruitee B.V., Johan Huizingalaan 763A, 1066VH, Amsterdam, Netherlands.

Your Data Protection Claims and Rights

If we process your personal data, you are entitled to various data protection claims against us. You have the right,

  • To request information about the data stored about your person and its origin, the purpose of processing as well as the recipients or categories of recipients of the data (Art. 15 GDPR, Sec. 34 BDSG),

  • Under certain conditions, to request rectification, restriction of processing or erasure of your personal data (Art.16 - 18 GDPR, Sec.35 BDSG),

  • To request the transfer of your data to another controller (Art. 20 GDPR) and

  • To complain to us or to the competent supervisory authority about data processing (Art.77 GDPR).

You can also object to the further processing of your data if we process your data on the basis of a legitimate interest (Art. 6 (1) subpara. 1 point (f) GDPR). Since we do not process your data for advertising purposes, this requires a reason that arises from your particular situation. In the event of an objection, we will no longer process your personal data to which the objection relates from receipt during the subsequent verification and – in the event of a justified objection – delete it after completion of the verification (Sec. 36 BDSG, Art. 21 GDPR).

If you transmit your consent to data processing (Art. 6 (1) subpara. 1 point (a) GDPR) to us, you can withdraw it at any time for no reason; we will then not process your personal data further unless we have legal permission to do so.

A justified objection and a withdrawal of your consent have no influence on data processing processes that have already taken place.

We fulfil all rights to which you are entitled free of charge and without delay. If you have any further questions, please contact us directly or our data protection officer using the contact details provided at the beginning.

﻿Imprint﻿
